## CADO-NFS 3.3.0-modern

The cycle after `3.2.0-modern`, on the fork's own minor line. It keeps upstream
[CADO-NFS](https://gitlab.inria.fr/cado-nfs/cado-nfs) **3.0.0**'s NFS mathematics,
numerics, and parameters **unchanged**. It opens on the conclusion the prior three
revisions measured, and re-measured, repeatedly: **on this reference hardware
(i9-10850K, Comet Lake — AVX2 but no AVX-512; one RTX 3090) single-machine NFS
*speed* is essentially tapped out.** CPU tuning is saturated; GPU cofactorization is
Amdahl-capped; GPU lattice sieving is a measured negative; the AVX-512 kernels are
silicon-gated; multi-GPU/multi-node are correctness-validated only at the degenerate
path. A 2026 survey (upstream CADO, msieve, YAFU, FLINT, GMP-ECM/CGBN, the
RSA-250/DLP-240 record papers) confirms no published technique since ~2010 yields a
>5 % single-machine speedup, and that this fork is already ahead of public
implementations on GPU + SIMD modernization.

> **No NFS mathematics is changed, and nothing ships unvalidated.** So 3.3.0 splits,
> transparently, into a **shippable, *measurable* operator-experience core** (the real
> here-and-now value, all of which runs on this box) and an **honestly-gated
> experimental/research track** attempted under the standing gate — `make check` +
> verified `product == N`, a bit-exact check vs GMP / the CPU path, or Intel-SDE
> validation — and **documented even when the outcome is a wash or a HW-gated
> design.** Honest negatives are a *valid deliverable*, not a failure.

There is **no NFS-algorithm or CPU-path change** in 3.3.0; the default build behaves
identically. The measurable wins are the operator experience, the first
*measured-on-silicon* AVX2 SIMD kernel, and Galois-automorphism detection.

### Usability / utility / help — the shippable core (E4–E8)

The genuine high-ROI center; all of it runs and helps on the reference box today.

- **E4 — live dashboard + ETA.** `cado-nfs-monitor-rs` (ratatui) now derives, from
  its own poll time-series, a **trailing-window ETA** + **throughput**
  (work-units/min) independent of what the server reports, plus local **host CPU**
  (`/proc/stat`) and **GPU** (`nvidia-smi`) utilisation. The dependency-free
  `/dashboard` HTML (Flask + Rust servers) mirrors the trend-ETA + throughput
  browser-side.
- **E5 — `--doctor` preflight.** `cado-nfs.py --doctor [N]` (+ `--doctor-json`): a
  **side-effect-free** check of the build (7 core binaries), CPU/SIMD, GPU, free
  RAM/disk vs the resource estimate for `N`, the Python env, and any cluster
  schedulers — printing a per-item `OK/info/WARN/FAIL` report and a single **GO /
  GO-with-warnings / NO-GO** verdict (`scripts/cadofactor/doctor.py`, doctested).
- **E6 — shell completions + man page.** bash/zsh/fish completions generated from
  the *single source of truth* — `cado-nfs.py`'s argparse spec — by
  `scripts/build-completions.py`, plus a `--completions <shell>` flag on all three
  Rust binaries (`clap_complete`) and a `misc/man/cado-nfs.1` man page; CMake
  installs all of them.
- **E7 — checkpoint/resume clarity.** Documented precisely what is resumable per
  phase (sieving is per-work-unit crash-safe via the SQLite DB; BWC krylov writes
  recoverable checkpoints), and surfaced the BWC checkpoint cadence as a first-class
  **`--checkpoint-interval`** flag.
- **E8 — Slurm/PBS integration.** A PBS/Torque `qsub` **job-array** path in
  `scripts/cluster-launch.sh` beside the existing Slurm `--sbatch` (shared
  `array_body` helper, GPU-pinned clients), plus **`--suggest-slurm-config` /
  `--suggest-pbs-config`** that emit a ready-to-edit submission script **sized to
  `N`** from the planner estimate. (`docs/usability-v330.md`.)

### AVX2 batched modular inverse — first measured-on-silicon SIMD (B4)

The fork's prior SIMD work (the AVX-512 B-series) is bit-exact under Intel SDE but
*silicon-gated* on Comet Lake. **B4 changes that.**

- The siever's per-prime 32-bit modular inverse (`invmod_redc_32`, the one
  genuinely-vectorizable hot slice) as an **AVX2 8-way masked binary-GCD**
  (`bench/avx2-modinv.c`), ported from the AVX-512 B1 kernel using **blend-based
  masking** (AVX2 has no k-mask registers). Because `U,V,X1,X2 < 2^31` the high bit
  is always clear, so signed AVX2 compares coincide with the unsigned compares the
  algorithm needs.
- It **runs natively** on the reference CPU, so it yields the fork's first
  *measured* batched-modinv SIMD result: **~4.6× the scalar binary-GCD** (193 → 42
  ns/inverse over 2^20 inverses), **bit-exact vs GMP — 0 / 320 000 wrong**, 0
  scalar-vs-AVX2 mismatches.
- Honest scope: end-to-end siever impact is **Amdahl-bounded** — the byte-scatter
  majority (~29 %) stays scalar (no 8-bit SIMD scatter), capping the whole-siever
  gain at ~1.05–1.10× even at 4.6× on this slice. Montgomery's batch-inversion trick
  does **not** apply (a different modulus per prime). (`docs/avx2-simd-b4.md`.)

### Galois automorphism auto-detection — a measurable algorithmic win (A5)

- A new exact detector (`scripts/cadofactor/galois.py`) finds whether the algebraic
  polynomial admits one of CADO's automorphisms (`autom2.1/2.2/3.1/3.2/4.1/6.1`) by
  testing **Möbius invariance in integer arithmetic** — `(c x + d)^deg · f(σ(x))` a
  scalar multiple of `f` — with the crucial `deg % order == 0` **orbit guard** (so
  `x²−x+1` does not falsely match an order-3 map).
- Exposed as **`cado-nfs.py --galois-detect POLYFILE`**, which prints the exact
  `--galois` flag to add (the quotient shrinks the matrix and sieve region up to the
  automorphism order) or a correct **no-op** for a generic GNFS polynomial.
- **Cross-validated against CADO's own `tests/sieve/galois.poly` fixture**
  (`autom2.2` for `x⁴ − 9x² + 101`) and a cyclic cubic (`autom3.1`); doctested
  (`test_python_galois`). Detection is **advisory** — the matrix/sieve reduction is
  CADO's existing, upstream-validated `--galois` feature. (`docs/galois-auto-a5.md`.)

### Experimental / research track — validated kernels, honest non-wins

Each item is attempted under the gate and reported with its honest scope.

- **C5 — GPU polyselect stage-2 root-sieve** (`bench/gpu-ropt-stage2.cu`): the root
  sieve (`rootsieve_run_line`) as an int32-accumulate scatter, **bit-exact vs the
  int16 CPU reference (0 wrong)** over a 4 M-cell line; ~1.7× on the raw apply step
  but an **honest wash** at testable sizes (real `ropt` sieves small per-rotation
  arrays → PCIe/launch-bound) — the win is large-N only. (`docs/gpu-polyselect-ropt-c5.md`.)
- **C6 — GPU GF(p) lingen NTT** (`bench/gpu-lingen-ntt.cu`): an iterative
  Cooley–Tukey NTT polynomial multiply over a 31-bit NTT prime, **bit-exact vs
  schoolbook (0 / 1199)**, ~0.5 ms for a degree-2^16 product. The single-prime inner
  transform of a multi-modular GF(p) lingen; lingen is ~3–8 % of BWC, so **<1 %
  single-machine net** — a multi-GPU/cluster-DLP play. (`docs/gpu-lingen-ntt-c6.md`.)
- **B5 — IFMA GF(p) → arith-modp routing bridge** (`bench/ifma-gfp.c`): the complete
  DLP routing arithmetic — radix-2^64 (arith-modp storage) ↔ radix-2^52 (IFMA)
  repack + the `vec_add_dotprod` `+w` addend — **bit-exact under Intel SDE
  (0 / 32 000)**. Honest: HW-gated (no IFMA silicon here) *and* repack-sensitive at
  the narrow p4/p5 widths; the in-tree specialization is the documented follow-up,
  **not committed** (it would be unvalidatable-on-this-box code). (`docs/ifma-gfp-b3.md`.)
- **A6 — exTNFS / Tower-NFS feasibility skeleton** (`docs/extnfs-a4.md`): extended
  the A4 study with **concrete interface sketches** (the tower field + polyselect,
  the (2η)-D special-q siever shape, tower-ideal/relation bookkeeping) — a documented
  design, no committed tower math.

### Compatibility & build

- **Drop-in for 3.2.0-modern.** The default build is unchanged (no NFS-algorithm or
  CPU-path change); `CADO_VERSION_STRING` is now `3.3.0-modern` (`CMakeLists.txt`
  `CADO_VERSION_MINOR 2 → 3`); no test depends on the string.
- New: completions install to the conventional per-shell locations and the man page
  to `share/man/man1/`; the Rust binaries gain `--completions`; `cargo fmt` clean.
  GPU benches need nvcc/CUDA 13; the AVX2 kernel runs on any AVX2 host (no SDE),
  while the AVX-512 kernels remain SDE-validated. Toolchain floor unchanged: C99 +
  C++20, CMake ≥ 3.18, GMP ≥ 5 `--enable-shared`.

### Validation summary

`product == N` end-to-end on the c59 smoke after all orchestration edits; the new
`scripts/cadofactor/{doctor,galois,planner}.py` doctests pass (`test_python_doctor`,
`test_python_galois`); **B4 AVX2 modinv bit-exact native (0/320000) at ~4.6×**; C5
bit-exact vs the int16 CPU reference (0 wrong); C6 bit-exact vs schoolbook (0/1199);
B5 bit-exact under Intel SDE (0/32000); A5 cross-validated vs CADO's `galois.poly`;
`cargo build --release` + `cargo fmt --check` + `clippy` clean; `ruff` clean on the
new Python. The two `make check` reds (`test_python_cadotask`, `test_python_toplevel`)
are a **pre-existing** environment quirk — ctest runs doctests under the system
`python3`, which lacks Flask (imported by `api_server.py`); both pass under the venv.
See `CHANGELOG.md`, `BENCHMARKS.md` §7, `docs/ROADMAP-v3.3.0-modern.md`, and the
per-track docs in `docs/` for the full methodology and honest scope of every item.

---

*This is a modernization + performance fork, **not** the official CADO-NFS. For
academic use, cite the upstream project (see `AUTHORS`). Released under LGPL-2.1,
preserving all upstream copyright and authorship. Prior releases: `v3.2.0-modern`,
`v3.1.0-modern`, `v3.0.0-modern`, `v2.3.1-modern`.*
